div.p: 1189: 48 8b 45 f0 mov rax,QWORD PTR [rbp-0x10] 118d: 48 8b 55 f8 mov rdx,QWORD PTR [rbp-0x8] 1191: 48 8d 4a 10 lea rcx,[rdx+0x10] 1195: c5 fc 10 00 vmovups ymm0,[rax] 1199: c5 fc 10 4a 20 vmovups ymm1,[rdx+0x20] 119e: c5 f4 58 c1 vaddps ymm0,ymm1,ymm0 11a2: c5 fc 11 07 vmovups [rdi],ymm0 11a6: 48 83 c7 20 add rdi,0x20 11aa: 75 e9 jne 1195

ul.li: Scan for mm prefix (SSE) Found in sample:

div.p: SIMD Database Lookup: vmovups → load, "AVX unaligned load" vaddps → compute, "AVX add 8 floats" vmovups → store, "AVX unaligned store"

ul.li: Match address ranges to source lines Result: 0x1195-0x11aa → loop body Source line 42: dst[i] = a[i] + b[i]
Slight latency penalty on older CPUs

div.p: 1195: vmovups ymm0,[rax] # [load] AVX unaligned load 1199: vmovups ymm1,[rdx+0x20] # [load] AVX unaligned load 119e: vaddps ymm0,ymm1,ymm0 # [compute] AVX add 8 floats 11a2: vmovups [rdi],ymm0 # [store] AVX unaligned store 11a6: add rdi,0x20 # [loop] stride 32 bytes 11aa: jne 1195 # [branch] loop back
